name: Annovis Bio, Inc.
business: Annovis Bio, Inc. is a late-stage clinical drug platform company developing buntanetap, a small molecule oral drug candidate for Alzheimer's disease and Parkinson's disease. The drug is designed to inhibit multiple neurotoxic proteins and is currently in a pivotal Phase 3 trial for early Alzheimer's disease and an open-label extension study for Parkinson's disease.

exposures.sentence: We are a late-stage clinical drug platform company addressing neurodegeneration, such as Alzheimer s disease ( AD ) and Parkinson s disease ( PD ).

catalysts.event: Initiation of open-label extension study for Parkinson's disease
catalysts.sentence: In January of 2026, we began an OLE study to evaluate the long-term safety and efficacy of buntanetap in PD patients.

catalysts.event: Readout of 6-month symptomatic component of Phase 3 AD trial
catalysts.sentence: We expect the 6-month symptomatic component of the combined AD trial to read-out in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Readout of 18-month disease modifying component of Phase 3 AD trial
catalysts.sentence: The 18-month disease modifying component is expected to read out in the second half of 2027.

tamClaims.claim: 55 million victims of AD or some form of dementia exist in the world and by 2050, the number will grow to nearly 140 million people worldwide.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that currently 55 million victims of AD or some form of dementia exist in the world and by 2050, the number will grow to nearly 140 million people worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: In the United States, it is estimated that there are nearly seven million patients with AD currently and that by 2060, the number of people aged 65 and older with AD is projected to reach nearly 14 million people.
tamClaims.sentence: In the United States, it is estimated that there are nearly seven million patients with AD currently and that by 2060, the number of people aged 65 and older with AD is projected to reach nearly 14 million people.

tamClaims.claim: PD affects about 1% of the population over the age of 60, while in individuals over the age of 85, this prevalence reaches 5%, PD affects more than 10 million people worldwide, of which nearly one million are in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: PD affects about 1% of the population over the age of 60, while in individuals over the age of 85, this prevalence reaches 5%, highlighting the impact that advancing age has on the risk of developing this condition. PD affects more than 10 million people worldwide, of which nearly one million are in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: The National Parkinson Foundation estimates that the economic burden of PD is at least $25 billion a year in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: The National Parkinson s Foundation estimates that the economic burden of PD is at least $25 billion a year in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: The current global market for PD drugs is estimated at $5 to $6 billion worldwide.
tamClaims.sentence: The current global market for PD drugs is estimated at $5 to $6 billion worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: By 2050, the number of worldwide PD patients is expected to grow to up to 30 million.
tamClaims.sentence: By 2050, the number of worldwide PD patients is expected to grow to up to 30 million.
